# nostr-attestations

> One Nostr event kind for all attestations — credentials, endorsements,
> vouches, provenance, licensing, and trust. Differentiated by a `type` tag,
> not by inventing new event kinds.

nostr-attestations is a zero-dependency TypeScript library implementing NIP-VA
(kind 31000 Verifiable Attestations). It provides builders, parsers, validators,
and frozen test vectors for creating and consuming attestation events on Nostr.

No relay client, no signing library, no crypto — bring your own. Works with
nostr-tools, any Nostr SDK, or bare WebSocket code.

## Getting Started

```
npm install nostr-attestations
```

```typescript
import { createAttestation, TYPES } from 'nostr-attestations'

// Create an unsigned attestation event template
const event = createAttestation({
  type: TYPES.CREDENTIAL,
  identifier: '<subject-pubkey>',
  subject: '<subject-pubkey>',
  summary: 'Professional credential verified',
  expiration: 1735689600,
  tags: [['profession', 'attorney'], ['jurisdiction', 'US-NY']],
  content: JSON.stringify({ proof: '...' }),
})
// => { kind: 31000, tags: [...], content: '...' }
// Sign with your preferred library and publish to relays
```

## Key Concepts

- **One kind, many types** — kind 31000 with a `type` tag. New attestation types need zero protocol changes.
- **Builders** — `createAttestation()` and `createRevocation()` return unsigned `EventTemplate` objects.
- **Parsers** — `parseAttestation()` extracts typed data from any `NostrEvent`. `isRevoked()` checks revocation status.
- **Validators** — `validateAttestation()` checks structural correctness (required tags, d-tag format).
- **Filters** — `attestationFilter()` and `revocationFilter()` build Nostr relay query filters.
- **D-tag convention** — `<type>:<identifier>` ensures one attestation per publisher per type per subject.
- **Revocation** — re-publish with `["status", "revoked"]`. Addressable event semantics handle the rest.
- **Validity windows** — optional `valid_from` + `expiration` tags define when an attestation is active.
- **Application profiles** — applications define their own types, tags, and content schemas.

## API Surface

- `createAttestation(params)` — build unsigned attestation event
- `createRevocation(params)` — build unsigned revocation event
- `parseAttestation(event)` — parse NostrEvent into typed Attestation
- `isRevoked(event)` — check if event is a revoked attestation
- `validateAttestation(event)` — structural validation
- `buildDTag(type, identifier)` — construct d-tag string
- `parseDTag(dTag)` — parse d-tag into type and identifier
- `attestationFilter(params)` — build relay query filter
- `revocationFilter(type, identifier)` — build revocation check filter
- `isValid(event, now?)` — check validity (revocation, expiration, validity windows)
- `buildAssertionDTag(ref)` — construct assertion d-tag string
- `ATTESTATION_KIND` — 31000
- `TYPES` — well-known type constants (credential, endorsement, vouch, verifier, provenance)

## Use Cases

- Identity verification (credentials, vouches, verifier registration)
- Professional licensing (regulatory credentials with jurisdiction tags)
- Product provenance (authenticity attestations with chain-of-custody)
- Peer endorsement (bilateral trust relationships)
- Service reputation (provider endorsements across marketplaces)

## Optional

- [NIP-VA.md](https://github.com/forgesworn/nostr-attestations/blob/main/NIP-VA.md): full NIP specification
- [README](https://github.com/forgesworn/nostr-attestations/blob/main/README.md): full API reference
- [vectors/](https://github.com/forgesworn/nostr-attestations/tree/main/vectors): 20 frozen conformance test vectors
